quranicRelevance: Theft (Saraqa - 'was-sariqu was-sariqatu'), and listening in secret (Istaraqa - 'illa man istaraqa as-sam'a').

keyDerivations.meaning: To steal, thieve, or take stealthily
To steal away, eavesdrop, or listen in secret

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِلَّا مَنِ اسْتَرَقَ السَّمْعَ فَأَتْبَعَهُ شِهَابٌ مُبِينٌ (15:18): Except he who steals a hearing/eavesdrops (demons trying to intercept cosmic revelations).
discardedNoise: Juristic debates on the minimum monetary threshold (Nisab) for hand cutting.
summaryEn: The root denotes theft and stealth, outlining the criminal law of theft (Saraq) and the cosmic shooting stars that block devilish eavesdropping (Istiraq).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على السرقة والاستلاب خفية، واصفاً حد السرقة الشرعي، والشهب الراصدة لاستراق السمع الفلكي من الشياطين.
rawText: استرق 8 : see 1, first sentence: === and see 3, in two places. [See also كَبِيسٌ.] === Also ‡ He deceived, or circumvented, secretly, [or by stealth,] like him who [so] listens. (TA.) === And you say, استرق الكَاتِبُ بَعْضَ المُحَاسَبَاتِ ‡ The writer suppressed some of the items of the reckoning. (TA.) انسرق 7 He went, drew, or shrank, back, in order to go away, عَنْهُمْ from them. (K, TA. [In this and the following sense, the verb is erroneously written in the CK اَسْرَقَ.]) === And He was, or became, languid, and weak, or feeble. (O, K, TA.) See also 1, last sentence. تسرّق 5 He stole [by degrees, or] one thing and then another. (O, K.) So in the phrase تسرّق شِعْرِى [He stole my poetry, bit by bit], used by Ru-beh. (O, TA.) === See also 3, in two places. سرّقهُ 2 : see 1. === Also, (S,) inf. n. تَسْرِيقٌ, (K,) He attributed to him [or accused him of] theft. (S.) It is said in the Kur [xii. 81], accord. to one reading, إِنَّ ٱبْنَكَ سُرِّقَ [Verily thy son has been accused of theft]. (S.) سَارِقٌ [Stealing; a thief; or] one who comes clandestinely to a place of custody, and takes what does not belong to him: (O:) pl. سَرَقَةٌ and سُرَّاقٌ (TA) and سُرَّقٌ. (Mgh.) سَارِقَةٌ sing. of سَوَارِقُ, which signifies [Collars by means of which the two hands are confined together to the neck, called also] جَوَامِعُ, (O, K, TA,) of iron, attached to fetters or shackles. (TA.) === And the pl., سَوَارِقُ, signifies also The adjuncts (زَوَائِد) in the catches (فَرَاش [q. v.]) of a lock. (Ibn-'Abbád, O, K.) سَرَقٌ Oblong pieces (S, O, Msb, * K) of silk; (S, O, Msb;) accord. to A'Obeyd, (S, O,) of white silk: (S, O, K:) or silk in general: (K:) said by A'Obeyd to be arabicized from the Pers. سَرَهْ, meaning “ good: ” (S, O:) n. un. with ة; (S, O, Msb;) which is expl. as meaning a piece of good silk. (TA.) سَرُوقٌ [Thievish; a great thief]; an epithet applied to a man, and to a dog: pl. سُرُقٌ. (TA.) سَرُوقَةٌ [Very thievish; a very great thief]: it has no pl. (TA.) سَرِقٌ and 🡻سَرِقَةٌ [the former of which is said in the Mgh and K, and the latter in the K, to be an inf. n., are also said to be] substs. from سَرَقَ, [as such signifying Theft,] as also 🡻سَرْقَةٌ, (O, K,) or 🡻سِرْقَةٌ. (Msb.) سَرِقَةٌ : see سَرِقٌ. === Also, (Msb,) A thing stolen; (Mgh, Msb;) and so 🡻سُرَاقَةٌ; [pl. of the latter سُرَاقَاتٌ;] whence the saying عِنْدَهُ سُرَاقَاتُ الشِّعْرِ [He has stolen things of poetry or verse]. (TA.) سَرْقَةٌ : see what next precedes. سَرْقَنَ الأَرْضَ He manured the land with سِرْقِين. (L in art. سرقن.) سُرَاقَةٌ : see سَرِقَةٌ. === Also A stealer of poetry or verses. (TA.) سُورَقٌ A certain disease in the members, or limbs. (Ibn-'Abbád, O.) سِرْقَةٌ : see what next precedes. سِرْقِينٌ , (K, and S and Msb in art. سرج,) sometimes written سَرْقِينٌ, (K,) as also سَرْجِين, (Msb, TA,) Dung of horses or other solid-hoofed animals, syn. رَوْثٌ, and زِبْلٌ, (Msb,) or fresh dung of camels, sheep and goats, wild oxen, and the like; (TA in art. ذأر;) a manure for land: (L:) arabicized from سركين [or سَرْگِينْ], (Msb, K,) a Pers. word. (Msb.) [See سِرْجِينٌ, in art. سرج.] مَسْرُوقُ الصَّوْتِ [lit. Having the voice stolen,] means ‡ hoarse in voice. (Z, TA.) And hence, مَسْرُوقُ البُغَامِ ‡ [A young gazelle] having a nasal sound, or twang, in its cry; as though its voice were stolen: a phrase used by El-Aashà. (TA.) مُسْتَرِقٌ ‡ Listening by stealth, (K, TA,) like the thief. (TA.) === † Defective, weak in make. (Ibn-'Abbád, O, K.) === مُسْتَرِقُ القَوْلِ ‡ Weak in speech or saying. (A, TA.) === مُسْتَرِقُ العُنُقِ ‡ Short in the neck; (Ibn-'Abbád, O, K, TA;) applied to a man; (Ibn-'Abbád, O, TA;) contracted therein. (A, TA.) [In the CK, المُسْرِقُ is erroneously put for المُسْتَرِقُ.] هُوَ يُسَارِقُ النَّظَرَ إِلَيْهِ 3 ‡ He avails himself of, (S, O,) or seeks, (K,) his inadvertence, to look at him: (S, O, K:) [he takes an opportunity of looking at him by stealth:] and in like manner one uses the phrases 🡻اِسْتَراقُالنَّظَرِ and 🡻تَسَرُّقُهُ [as meaning ‡ the taking an opportunity of looking by stealth]: and 🡻التَّسَرُّقُ [alone] signifies † the taking an opportunity of looking and of hearing: (TA:) [and the hearing discourse by stealth; as is indicated in the TA:] and 🡻استرقالسَّمْعَ [and استرق alone, as appears from an explanation of the part. n. مُسْتَرِقٌ, below,] ‡ He listened, (S, O,) or heard, (Msb,) by stealth; (S, O, Msb;) as also 🡻سَرَقَالسَّمْعَ. (Msb.)
